page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical Intel cores operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server type includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, providing high-speed, low-latency local disk access directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8idb.4xlarge balances dedicated compute resources, a standard memory-to-vCPU ratio, and integrated high-speed local storage. This configuration is designed for workloads requiring a combination of compute, memory, and fast local temporary storage, such as relational databases, caching layers, backend application servers, and build environments.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical Intel cores operating at 3.9 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, which delivers low-latency local disk access. The instance also provides a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. Lacking dedicated GPUs, this server is designed for workloads that require a balance of compute power, memory, and fast local storage. Appropriate use cases include database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose application serving.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
